methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Prusa Research is a bootstrapped manufacturer in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, founded by Josef Průša. The relevant peer universe comprises 11 sector peers across public and private markets—1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer set lacks sufficient granularity for traditional comparable company analysis.

The Peer-Median method is inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables. Screening the 11 Robotics & Manufacturing peers for matching stage, proximity, or valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints in any filtered category. An all-sector median approach would introduce material bias given the heterogeneity of robotics subsectors, automation tiers, and capital structures. The absence of a tight peer cohort—particularly other bootstrapped or early-stage manufacturing automation firms with comparable revenue and growth trajectory—precludes reliable triangulation via this path.

Without sufficient comparable transaction data or a robust peer valuation matrix, standard income and market approaches cannot be anchored to observable benchmarks in this segment. Any valuation must rest on bespoke analysis of Prusa's unit economics, customer acquisition costs, gross margins, and capital efficiency relative to the broader set of 11 peers, supplemented by expert judgment on the founder's execution risk and market positioning. The analysis is constrained by data availability rather than methodological choice.
